subject: LU-12473 llapi: fix pool_list by path

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

3 style warning(s).

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Thanks for the patch. Rather than using llapi_search_mounts(), which is inefficient, it would be better to use llapi_getname() instead, which only has one ioctl() to the kernel rather than several read calls and complex parsing of /etc/fstab. Bonus points if you add a man page for that function.

Please also add a sanity test case for this usage to verify that it is working correctly and does not break in the future.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

ok, I just looked at other similar functions that used llapi_search_mounts ; will make the switch.

That said cost-wise I'm not sure which really is more expensive - the ioctl needs to open the fs root directory, that means taking a lock if it's not already present and a few RPCs with the first MDS ; llapi_search_mount on the other hand is more complex userspace-wise but is 100% node-local so will probably end up being faster.

Anyway, pool_list is not a fast path operation, I don't think it really matters and will change that and add a test.

Patch Set 2:

> That said cost-wise I'm not sure which really is more expensive -
 > the ioctl needs to open the fs root directory, that means taking a
 > lock if it's not already present and a few RPCs with the first MDS
 > ; llapi_search_mount on the other hand is more complex
 > userspace-wise but is 100% node-local so will probably end up being
 > faster.

Opening the root directory doesn't need an RPC, since that dentry is pinned on the client.  Running "strace lfs getname myth" intermingled with the kernel debug log (+vfstrace+rpctrace) shows no RPCs:

 1561635891.186468 open("/myth", O_RDONLY|O_NONBLOCK) = 3 <0.000174>
 1561635891.186550:0:28585:0:(file.c:3661:__ll_inode_revalidate()) VFS Op:inode=[0x208001:0x1c08b680:0x0](ffff8803c8d7dc08),name=/
 1561635891.186580:0:28585:0:(file.c:4043:ll_inode_permission()) VFS Op:inode=[0x208001:0x1c08b680:0x0](ffff8803c8d7dc08), inode mode 41ed mask 44
 1561635891.186588:0:28585:0:(file.c:490:ll_file_open()) VFS Op:inode=[0x208001:0x1c08b680:0x0](ffff8803c8d7dc08), flags 104000
 1561635891.186810 ioctl(3, 0xc0086683, 0x7ffd417b1fa0) = 0 <0.000208>
 1561635891.186888:0:28585:0:(dir.c:1163:ll_dir_ioctl()) VFS Op:inode=[0x208001:0x1c08b680:0x0](ffff8803c8d7dc08), cmd=0xc0086683
 1561635891.187158 close(3)              = 0 <0.000098>
 1561635891.187219:0:28585:0:(file.c:315:ll_file_release()) VFS Op:inode=[0x208001:0x1c08b680:0x0](ffff8803c8d7dc08)

Compare that to the strace of the llapi_search_mounts() call:

 1561636280.518441 open("/proc/mounts", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC) = 3 <0.000122>

 1561636280.519554 read(3, "", 1024)     = 0 <0.000045>

 1561636280.527628 openat(AT_FDCWD, "/proc/fs/lustre/lov", O_RDONLY|O_NONBLOCK|O_DIRECTORY|O_CLOEXEC) = 3 <0.000090>

until it has the filesystem name...

Patch Set 2:

> Opening the root directory doesn't need an RPC, since that dentry
 > is pinned on the client.  Running "strace lfs getname myth"
 > intermingled with the kernel debug log (+vfstrace+rpctrace) shows
 > no RPCs:

That's only true if you have the lock, as I said -- most client will have it most of the time, but if any client in the cluster try a mkdir on the root then other clients will need to get it back.

I got this vfstrace+rpctrace log with a 2 mounts llmount:

 # mkdir /mnt/lustre2/foo
 # lctl dk > /dev/null
 # lfs getname /mnt/lustre
 # lctl dk

Anyway, this is fairly moot - I agree most of the time it won't need refreshing.
I still prefer something 100% local even if it's more syscalls than something that can sometimes need network, but this isn't performance critical at all so I don't really care.

Bisect in progress to update commit message, I'll push changed function update together when it's done

parents.subject: LU-4423 ptlrpc: remove inline on non-inlined functions.

commit.subject: LU-12473 llapi: fix pool_list by path
commit.message:
LU-12473 llapi: fix pool_list by path

lfs/lctl pool_list <fs_path> would print the FS path as pool prefix.
print fsname properly instead.

Fixes: 8813fdf2a4f2 ("LU-5030 util: migrate liblustreapi to use cfs_get_paths()")
Change-Id: I016b794fabd3d161d4651b41989637aebdf31f36
Signed-off-by: Dominique Martinet <dominique.martinet@cea.fr>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/35320
